label: Proto-Indo-European
definition: Earliest phase of linguistic unity of all Indo-European branches, including Anatolian and Tocharian, ending with fragmentation of the late Indo-European dialect continuum.

label: Fragmented Indo-European
definition: Proto-Indo-European dialects have broken off from the original dialect continuum but still lack evidence of the major sound laws characterizing specifically Celtic or Germanic.

label: After Proto-Celtic sound changes
definition: Borrowings after sound changes leading up to Proto-Celtic, e.g. PIE *ē > PC *ī, PIE *p > PC *ϕ (> *0), PIE *sn > PC *nn.

label: After major Proto-Germanic consonant shifts
definition: Borrowings after Grimm’s law, Verner’s law, and Kluge’s law; somewhere in this stratum Pre-Germanic *o > PG *a and Pre-Germanic *ā > PG *ō also occur.

label: Fragmented Germanic
definition: Begins with breakup of Proto-Germanic into separate dialects; lexical exchange in this phase falls outside the temporal scope of the study.
